perturbation.original_text: 1.6 "CUSTOMER(S)" means one or more customers of the Corio Services having its principal executive offices in the Territory who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software or MarketSite.net Service, in the Corio Market Segment.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 "CLIENT(S)" means one or more customers of the Corio Services having its principal executive offices in the Territory who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software or MarketSite.net Service, in the Corio Market Segment.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'CUSTOMER(S)' with 'CLIENT(S)' in the definition introduces a potential ambiguity. Throughout the document, 'Customer' is consistently used, now the introduction of 'Client' raises the question of whether these terms are interchangeable or refer to distinct entities. This ambiguity creates uncertainty in the interpretation and enforcement of clauses that refer to 'Customer'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.5 "CORIO SERVICES" means the hosting services offered by Corio to its Customers in which Corio allows Customers to access the Corio Servers.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'CUSTOMER(S)' to 'CLIENT(S)' introduces a potential ambiguity, contradicting the consistent use of 'Customer' in the contradicted text ('Corio allows Customers to access the Corio Servers'). This misalignment of terminology, as per the definition, creates uncertainty as to whether 'Client' and 'Customer' are interchangeable, potentially conflicting with the obligations related to 'Customers' as defined in section 1.5. This makes enforcement unclear. Therefore, it is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 Support and Maintenance. Commerce One shall provide Corio with support described in EXHIBIT C hereto, and maintenance in the form of Updates and Upgrades. Corio shall be responsible for providing its Customers with routine technical support of the Software and MarketSite.net Service. Corio shall escalate any technical support questions or problems it is unable to answer or resolve directly to Commerce One for Commerce One's immediate attention and resolution under the schedule set forth in EXHIBIT C hereto. The support described in this Section 5.3 and EXHIBIT C hereto shall be provided to Corio but Commerce One shall have no obligation to provide any maintenance or support services to other third parties. Subject to Corio's payment of the annual support and maintenance fee, Commerce One's support and maintenance obligation of the Software and MarketSite.net Service shall continue after termination or expiration of this Agreement with respect to all Software Users granted access to the Software and MarketSite.net Service prior to termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 Support and Maintenance. Commerce One shall provide Corio with support described in EXHIBIT C hereto, and maintenance in the form of Updates and Upgrades. Corio shall be responsible for providing its Users with routine technical support of the Software and MarketSite.net Service. Corio shall escalate any technical support questions or problems it is unable to answer or resolve directly to Commerce One for Commerce One's immediate attention and resolution under the schedule set forth in EXHIBIT C hereto. The support described in this Section 5.3 and EXHIBIT C hereto shall be provided to Corio but Commerce One shall have no obligation to provide any maintenance or support services to other third parties. Subject to Corio's payment of the annual support and maintenance fee, Commerce One's support and maintenance obligation of the Software and MarketSite.net Service shall continue after termination or expiration of this Agreement with respect to all Software Users granted access to the Software and MarketSite.net Service prior to termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Customers' to 'Users' in Section 5.3 introduces confusion because 'Software User' is a defined term (1.1) referring to a specific licensed user, while 'Customer' (1.6) refers to an entity that obtains a sublicense. This change creates uncertainty whether Corio is responsible for supporting all individual users or only the entities holding the sublicense, creating ambiguity and enforcement challenges.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 "CLIENT(S)" means one or more customers of the Corio Services having its principal executive offices in the Territory who obtains a sublicense from Corio to use the Software or MarketSite.net Service, in the Corio Market Segment.
perturbation.justification: The original text states Corio is responsible for providing its 'Customers' with routine technical support. 'Customers' are defined in 1.6 as entities that obtain a sublicense. The changed text replaces 'Customers' with 'Users.' While Software Users are defined, this change broadens Corio's support obligations to include all individual users, not just the sublicensing entities, contradicting the definition of CLIENT(S) in 1.6 and creating an ambiguous obligation.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 Distribution License: Corio shall have the right to resell licenses for Commerce One software, including Hosted BuySite, to any Corio Customer in the Territory, [*]. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Commerce One hereby grants to Corio a nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement), right and license in the Territory to sell and distribute such software licenses to Customers pursuant to this Section 2.4. Under no circumstances shall Commerce One contact Corio Customers regarding a non-ASP license sale, unless requested to do so by Corio. Further, if a Corio Customer contacts Commerce One to purchase the Software license independent of the Corio Services, Commerce One shall immediately refer that Customer to Corio.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 Distribution License: Corio shall have the right to resell licenses for Commerce One software, including Hosted BuySite, to any Corio Client in the Territory, [*]. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Commerce One hereby grants to Corio a nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement), right and license in the Territory to sell and distribute such software licenses to Customers pursuant to this Section 2.4. Under no circumstances shall Commerce One contact Corio Customers regarding a non-ASP license sale, unless requested to do so by Corio. Further, if a Corio Customer contacts Commerce One to purchase the Software license independent of the Corio Services, Commerce One shall immediately refer that Customer to Corio.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 2.4, the term 'Corio Customer' has been changed to 'Corio Client' in the first sentence, while the rest of the paragraph still uses 'Corio Customer'. Given that 'Client' (defined in 1.6) and 'Customer' are now present without a clear definition of their relationship, this creates an in-text contradiction and leads to ambiguity in interpreting Corio's rights and obligations related to reselling licenses and Commerce One's contact restrictions. It is unclear whether the contact restriction applies to both Clients and Customers or only to Customers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distribution License: Corio shall have the right to resell licenses for Commerce One software, including Hosted BuySite, to any Corio Client in the Territory, [*]. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Commerce One hereby grants to Corio a nonexclusive, nontransferable (except in accordance with Section 14.1 of this Agreement), right and license in the Territory to sell and distribute such software licenses to Customers pursuant to this Section 2.4. Under no circumstances shall Commerce One contact Corio Customers regarding a non-ASP license sale, unless requested to do so by Corio. Further, if a Corio Customer contacts Commerce One to purchase the Software license independent of the Corio Services, Commerce One shall immediately refer that Customer to Corio.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Corio Customer' to 'Corio Client' in the first sentence of Section 2.4, while the rest of the paragraph continues to use 'Corio Customers,' creates an in-text contradiction. The definition of 'Client' in 1.6, combined with the continued use of 'Customer,' introduces ambiguity. The Contradicted Text in 2.4 is the same as the changed text and now has conflicting terminology creating a direct conflict. Because there are two different terms used, where it is unclear if the terms are the same or different, and the Contradicted Text includes both terms, there is a clear contradiction. Thus, this meets the definition of misaligned terminology leading to an in-text contradiction, because it leads to conflicting obligations. This satisfies the condition for a 'YES' answer.
